summary: ulid-xyz impersonates the ulid/ulidx library namespace
details: Package is published as `ulid-xyz` but ships a verbatim copy of the `ulidx` library's API, sets `homepage` and `bugs` to github.com/ulid/javascript (the legitimate upstream), and declares the original ulidx author in the `author` field. The runtime code in `dist/` is the genuine ULID implementation: no install lifecycle hooks, no network calls, no filesystem reads, no `exec`/`spawn`. However, `package.json` declares `pino`, `ws`, `esbuild`, `postinstall`, `ts-node`, `tsup`, `zod`, `@types/node`, `@types/ws`, and `typescript` as runtime `dependencies` — none of which are imported by the shipped code — so `npm install ulid-xyz` pulls a large unrelated transitive tree into the consumer's environment. Name-confusion against an established library combined with an inflated, unjustified dependency closure warrants human review of maintainer intent before recommending the package, but the current version contains no concrete installer-harm payload (no exfiltration, no dropper, no silent-relay, no credential distribution).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
